video_title: Top #DDD Videos in Las Vegas | Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives | Food Network

restaurants.name: Crepe Expectations
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here on the southeast side of Las Vegas, Nevada, right on the border of Henderson, Nevada. This is Crepe Expectations.

dishes.description: Crepe filled with marinated and cooked terrace major beef, homemade salsa, and guacamole, folded like a quesadilla.
dishes.guy_response: Oh. Right? That carne asada and that salsa with the guac. Is there a Mexican restaurant in this village that you're running out of town? Because that's what that tastes like. Dig it. The crepe, fantastic. Nice and tender. A little bit of cheese. Lots of meat. Dig it. That citrus, the carne asada, is fantastic. Awesome fire roasted salsa. Well done, man.

restaurants.name: Marsigliano's Pizzeria and More
restaurants.guy_intro: So Hunter and I are right off the strip in Las Vegas. This is Marsigliano's Pizzeria and More.

dishes.description: Fried chicken breast, marinara sauce, mozzarella, and ricotta cheese wrapped in pizza dough and baked, then topped with garlic butter and parmesan.
dishes.guy_response: Great crust. I'm going to take a bite. Why can't I be part of this? You're not from Triple D land. Yes, I am. Yes, I am. The onion, it sings all the way through. Gives you that little kick. It's very hearty. And the marinara, nice, fresh, clean. I think it's dynamite.

restaurants.name: House of Dutch Pot
restaurants.guy_intro: Well, that's why I bring you to this place. I'm hearing from everybody that this place is doing authentic Caribbean. This is House of Dutch Pot.

dishes.dish_name: Jerk Chicken with Rice and Peas
dishes.description: Grilled jerk chicken, marinated with a blend of Jamaican spices, served with a sweet and tangy tamarind jerk sauce and traditional rice and peas cooked with coconut milk and red kidney beans.
dishes.guy_response: Oh my god. The tamarind jerk sauce. But it is that sweet and that tangy. The chicken's another level. That is some of the greatest chicken. I can't get it in my mouth fast enough. Jerk chicken looking good. It's just so flavorful, so tender. The tamarind is amazing. I love the sweetness and the sourness.

restaurants.name: Fat Joy Asian American Fair
restaurants.guy_intro: I think everybody knows by now I went to college here in Las Vegas. And I'm about a half mile off the strip. Now, I drove by this place a million times. Never stopped in here. It's called the Eureka. Casino. And boy, did I miss out. This is the home of Fat Joy Asian American Fair.

dishes.description: Chinese steamed bun filled with marinated, baked, and seared pork belly, hoisin sauce, sauteed preserved mustard greens, crushed peanuts, and cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Great steamed bun, nice, tender, nice, and light. The pork is tender, sweet. I like the counterbalance with the luster. The luster green, really good, man. Awesome. I feel ill. Nice. All right, pork belly bell. Luscious and delicious and outstanding. It's like the ultimate slider, right? It's like super-sized bacon.

restaurants.name: John Moll's Meats and Roadkill Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: Now I'll give you a little bit of history. You know, I've worked at some butcher shops. When I was 12 growing up in Ferndale, I worked at Ferndale Meats making the jerky. I moved here to Las Vegas to go to college. I worked at Shulman's Meats. So when I find out there's a 70-year-old butcher shop up here in Northwest Las Vegas that's making their own sauce, their own rubs, their own jerky, their own sausage, their own barbecue, and they're serving it in their own backyard, I just got to check it out. This is John Moll's Meats and Roadkill Grill.

dishes.description: Spicy sausage made from ground shoulder claw pork, seasoned with a blend of spices including cayenne and hickory smoke, cured, applewood smoked, and then grilled.
dishes.guy_response: When it snaps, you know it's good. That snaps. The hot link is outstanding. This ain't no hot dog. I'm such a hot link fan. This has got to be one of the best. Not just on Triple D. I'm talking in general. Outstanding.

restaurants.name: Guy's Vegas Kitchen and Bar
restaurants.guy_intro: Welcome back, Mr. Fieri. Thank you. Listen, if you had ever told me that I was going to be on the Food Network, I'd say you're crazy. And if you ever told me I was going to come back to Las Vegas and open my own restaurant on the Strip, I'd say you're out of your mind. So I guess that makes you crazy and out of your mind? Because I'm here at the Link Hotel and Casino to check out my joint. This is Guy's Vegas Kitchen and Bar.

dishes.description: Smoked pulled pork, dry-rubbed with a blend of spices, served on a toasted pretzel roll with donkey sauce, melted cheddar, pickles, citrus slaw, and a mix of bourbon brown sugar and Carolina barbecue sauces.
dishes.guy_response: Mm, great smoke, a little bit of the sweet, a little bit of the vinegar, the crunch of the slaw, a little bit of that acid going through. Pickles, you've got to have the pickles. And then this nice pretzel bun with a little leathery skin on the outside, very nice. And your Motley Q sandwich? Absolutely tender and delicious. You get the sweet and the salty with it. It's really good.

dishes.description: A spicy Bloody Mary mix made with fresh peppers, garlic, tomato juice, and various sauces, strained, and then elaborately garnished with candied bacon, apple sausage, pepperoni, and fresh vegetables.
dishes.guy_response: This is exactly what you need after a rough night in Vegas. That'll do the trick. Guys, Bloody Mary. I love spice, so it tastes really good.
